commit.message:
fix: robust plan extraction from planner text output (#97)

## Problem
The deck orchestrator failed with **'Planner did not produce a valid
slide plan'** when the LLM output its plan as text instead of calling
\submit_plan\.

## Root Cause
All 3 plan extraction methods failed when the model didn't call the
tool:
1. \getLastPlan()\ — tool handler never ran
2. \xtractPlanFromEvents()\ — no \	ool.execution_start\ event
3. \parsePlanFromText()\ — lazy regex mis-matched nested JSON; only
handled \{slides:[…]}\

## Fix
- **\parsePlanFromText\**: bracket-counting JSON extractor (replaces
lazy regex); handles bare arrays and \{plan:{slides:…}}\ wrappers;
structured markdown parsing as final fallback
- **\coerceToPlan\**: normalizes diverse JSON shapes into \DeckPlan\
- **\PLANNER_PROMPT\**: explicit 'you MUST call submit_plan' + example +
'do NOT output as text' guardrail

## Tests
- 757 integration tests pass
- Typecheck clean

Co-authored-by: Stefan Broenner <stefan.broenner@microsoft.comm>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Resolve wchar_t→char narrowing UB in dictionary key conversion

IccHeuristicsLibrary.cpp:1610 — std::string(key.begin(), key.end())
triggered UBSAN implicit-conversion when wchar_t values >127 were
narrowed to char. Fixed with explicit static_cast<char>(unsigned char)

commit.message:
fix: strip English stop words from FTS query before building tsquery

'simple' pg config has no stop words, so connector words like 'and',
'the', 'or' become literal tokens that match almost every molecule —
corrupting ts_rank_cd scores and potentially causing query syntax errors
in some PGlite versions.

Fix: filter a 70-word English stop word set from query terms before
building the 'work | unemployment' tsquery string.  Falls back to full
term list if stripping removes everything (e.g. query is entirely stops).

Same tsTerms list reused for atom inflation (ContextInflator) so we
don't waste radial inflation budget on 'and' / 'the' atom positions.

Example: 'work and unemployment' -> terms: ['work','unemployment']
         tsquery: 'work | unemployment'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: Support weak symbol resolution for GCC-compiled libraries

GCC-compiled shared libraries contain weak symbols like __cxa_finalize,
__gmon_start__, _ITM_registerTMCloneTable, and _ITM_deregisterTMCloneTable.
These symbols are optional (marked STB_WEAK) and should resolve to NULL
if not found, per ELF specification.

Previously, the loader failed with 'undefined symbol' errors when loading
any GCC-compiled library, making it non-functional for its stated purpose.

Changes:
- Added symBind() helper to extract symbol binding type from ELF symbol table
- Modified resolveSymForReloc() to check if symbol is WEAK before failing
- Weak symbols now resolve to NULL (0) instead of causing load failure
- Added comprehensive integration tests for dl package

Impact:
- testdata/libtest.so now loads successfully (previously failed)
- testdata/libreloc.so now loads successfully
- Constructors/destructors execute correctly
- All GCC-compiled libraries can now be loaded

Testing:
- go test -race ./... passes
- CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./dl passes (6 tests)
- ./pgldd testdata/libtest.so succeeds and prints symbol table
- Complexity increase: resolveSymForReloc 6→7 (acceptable, under threshold)

Fixes audit finding: CRITICAL - Missing libc symbol resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 23H2 builds incorrectly labeled as 22H2

The preceding-sibling version detection loop was matching version strings
anywhere in the paragraph text. The 23H2 section has a note paragraph:
'Want to quickly update eligible Windows 11, version 22H2 devices to
Windows 11, version 23H2?'

Since PowerShell's -match is case-insensitive, the regex 'Version\s+(\d{2}H\d)'
matched 'version 22H2' in that note text (the closest preceding sibling),
capturing '22H2' instead of the correct '23H2' from the heading.

Fix: anchor the regex with '^' and trim the text so it only matches paragraphs
that START with 'Version', which excludes the note paragraph.

Fixes #5
